title: Rebuild trilogy in Paris France
wikitext:
[]: Rebuild trilogy in Paris, France             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Jun 06, 2013 4:53 pm The news just appeared yesterday : Dybex, the company releasing Evangelion in France, will throw a special even in the cinema Le Grand Rex , in Paris, when they will screen the whole Rebuild trilogy ! We don't know if it will be the subbed or dubbed version yet, nor the price. Many people think that Dybex will also announce a date for the release of the 3.33 DVD and BD, and maybe a pack with the trilogy! The event will be on June 29th . Just so you know, this is Le Grand Rex screening room : SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Rebuild trilogy in Paris, France             Quote Post by hui43210 » Thu Jun 06, 2013 5:44 pm ElMariachi wrote: The news just appeared yesterday : Dybex, the company releasing Evangelion in France, will throw a special even in the cinema Le Grand Rex , in Paris, when they will screen the whole Rebuild trilogy ! We don't know if it will be the subbed or dubbed version yet, nor the price. Many people think that Dybex will also announce a date for the release of the 3.33 DVD and BD, and maybe a pack with the trilogy! The event will be on June 29th . Just so you know, this is Le Grand Rex screening room : SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Ænimal » Thu Jun 06, 2013 5:45 pm je suis jaloux

[]:             Quote Post by Jurrasic » Thu Jun 06, 2013 6:06 pm You are a lucky, lucky bastard El Mariachi! (I assume you are going, anyway.) If you do, enjoy and tell us how the reactions were, eh?

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Thu Jun 06, 2013 7:51 pm You French bastards! I've always loved you! Too bad I can't find a possible gig to get me to France within the next few weeks. Curses!

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Jun 08, 2013 7:57 pm The french langue and I have never been good friends. ...which, now comes to bite me in the ass since I actually live right next to the french border and could technically go there by train... But no. I'll just get lost trying to find someone who speaks English to ask them for the way XD

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat Jun 29, 2013 6:57 pm Well, that's it, I just came back from the marathon, and... Holy Shit, these movies are made for the cinema, the experience is completely different, especially the sound : the music was much more present and coming from the screen, while the voices came from audio speakers in the side walls, that was magnificent! By the way the complains that the BR/DVD versions muffled the musics seems to be right, they are not nearly as present as in the theater version, which is a shame because they masterfully help in setting the mood of the scenes. I just have one word in my mind : Ramiel, the FUCKING Ramiel screams! The shrieks when he powered up were already destroying my ears, but the screams he did when being hit, that was utterly insane! The only things which got close to that level were Shinji's scream of rage when he thrown the S-DAT across the room and Asuka's screams of rage while fighting Shinji inside Lilith's Chamber in 3.0! I also took a lot of notes from the subtitles of 3.0(the first official non-Japanese subtitles), but right now I'm putting them in order while it's still fresh in my head, I will post everything tomorrow, but just the French subs made all the pilots extremely vulgars : Shinji's "I had enough!" just before calling for Rei in the Wunder was translated as a glorious "Allez tous vous faire foutre!" , which translate as "Go all fuck yourselves"... for real. Shinji Ikari. I'm not talking about Mari calling Rei Q "bitch" or Asuka swearing more than Revy from Black Lagoon! Truly French translations are an unique experience!

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Sat Jun 29, 2013 7:19 pm So the subs were shitty, but I wouldn't care, I could watch these films hundreds of times without the subs and I would enjoy them. That must have been really beautiful! :) The magic of film, with many people. I get very emotional watchin an Eva film on my PC, but still, watching this in a cinema must be magical. Something I'll maybe never do... Still, I am happy just imagining being in that cinema. Waiting for the film, watching the film, talking with your friends about it after the viewing... At least I'll watch the film with seven friends in a couple of days, and I have a pretty big 1080p TV. :) I want to be a filmmaker, really.

[]:             Quote Post by iihieiii » Sun Jun 30, 2013 1:07 am YAY congrats bro ! Can't wait for your next post then =D

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Mon Jul 01, 2013 3:37 pm The French know how to do things in grand style. Félicitations! I won't be having this same experience in 4 weeks, but you take what you can.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Jul 01, 2013 4:13 pm Oh yes, for honesty sake I need to make a little correction : I was wrong about the screening room, it wasn't the one in the image I posted in the OP(the main room), but in the Room 2 which still has an honorable capacity of 503 seats : SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Mon Jul 01, 2013 6:04 pm Still amazing!
